methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
MatX is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Reiner Pope that has raised $130M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including Positron AI, Etched, and d-Matrix on the private side and NVDA, AMD, and ALAB publicly.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $130M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $247M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a $1.0B median valuation, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. The peer set includes Positron AI at $1.0B (Series B), d-Matrix at $2.0B (Series A), Etched at $5.0B (Series A), Substrate at $1.0B (Series A), and DEEPX at $529M (Series C). Public-comp anchoring is not applicable given MatX's undisclosed revenue.

The triangulated valuation range is $210M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $624M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around MatX's capital efficiency, technology differentiation, and revenue trajectory relative to the peer set. The gap between stage-progression ($247M) and peer-median ($1.0B) estimates is substantial and warrants further diligence on the comparability of peer-stage classifications and MatX's competitive positioning within the frontier-chip market.
